login.subtitle: Log in to continue using Wakapi. First time? Check out the
login.signup_disabled: The administrator of this instance has disabled sign up.
signup.title: Sign up to Wakapi
signup.subtitle: Welcome to Wakapi! Your first step is to create an account. Afterwards, make sure to set up the WakaTime client tools as described in the
signup.email_hint: E-Mail address is optional, but required for weekly reports and password reset.
signup.first_user_note: Please note: Since there are no users registered in the system, yet, the first user will have administrative privileges, while additional users won't.
signup.signup_disabled: The administrator of this instance has disabled sign up.
settings.title: Settings
settings.timezone_desc: Time Zone, which you are located in. Relevant for displaying daily statistics.
settings.start_of_week_desc: Choose which day your week starts on. This affects "This Week" and "Last Week" intervals.
settings.email_desc: Optional in general, but required for weekly reports and for resetting your password.
settings.reports_weekly_desc: Opt in to receive a summary of your coding activity once a week.
settings.current_password_desc: Enter your old password for verification.
settings.new_password_desc: Choose a new password. Preferably, it is at least 8 characters long and contains letters, digits and special chars.
settings.invite_friends_desc: You can invite new users to Wakapi with an exclusive code. Click to generate one and then send the link to your friend. Thanks for spreading Wakapi to the world!
settings.unknown_projects_desc: You can choose to exclude and ignore coding stats from unknown projects. Changing this setting will require to recompute your statistics.
settings.aliases_desc: You can specify aliases for any type of entity. For instance, you can define a rule, that both "myapp-frontend" and "myapp-backend" are combined under a project called "myapp".
settings.project_labels_desc: You can assign labels (aka. tags) to projects to group them together, e.g. by "private" and "work".
settings.bulk_association_desc: Associate a label with multiple projects
settings.label: Label
settings.no_projects_yet: You don't have any projects, yet. Start out by sending a few heartbeats before you can then assign labels.
settings.language_mappings_desc: You can specify custom mapping from file extensions to programming languages, for instance a ".jsx" file could be mapped to the "React" language.
settings.heartbeats_timeout_desc: This parameter affects the heuristic based on which a series of consecutive heartbeats sent by your IDE are aggregated to total coding time. The lower the timeout value, the more pauses will be recognized (i.e. time during which you didn't type) and the lower your total coding time. A good default is 10 minutes.
settings.vibrant_colors_desc: You can view the entire summary in vibrant colors similar to the "Languages" chart. Note that this setting is saved in your web browser only.
settings.public_leaderboard_desc: Opt in to get listed in the public leaderboard. It shows aggregated statistics from the past 7 days of your coding.
settings.public_data_desc: Some features require public access to your data without authentication. This mainly includes badges ("shields" endpoint) and the integration with GitHub Readme Stats ("stats" endpoint). You can choose which data to share publicly through these endpoints.
settings.wakatime_desc: You can connect Wakapi with the official WakaTime to forward your heartbeats to WakaTime as well. Or, if you're migrating from WakaTime to Wakapi, you can import your historical data from there.
settings.wakatime_relay_desc: To forward data to another Wakapi instance, enter its URL (e.g. https://wakapi.dev/api) and your corresponding API key.
settings.wakatime_note: Please note: When enabling this feature, heartbeats are forwarded to WakaTime's (or another Wakapi's) API from the server-side. While using HTTPS in transit, your API key is stored unencrypted in the Wakapi database.
settings.wakatime_legacy_desc: If WakaTime import fails repeatedly, try enabling this option to use a slower but more compatible importer.
settings.badges_desc: This integration allows to generate badges for README pages or forums. They are powered by Shields.io.
settings.readme_stats_desc: Wakapi integrates with GitHub Readme Stats to show your coding activity on your GitHub profile.
settings.subscription_desc: By default, this Wakapi instance will only store historical coding activity for %d months.
settings.subscription_cancel: You can cancel your subscription at any time!
settings.subscription_without: Without a subscription, your coding activity older than %d months will get deleted.
settings.subscription_note_desc: If you just purchased a subscription, it might take a moment until it's active.
settings.subscription_tos: By purchasing a subscription, you agree to our terms of service.
settings.oldest_data: Your currently oldest data point is from
settings.subscription_email_required: You have to provide an e-mail address to purchase a subscription.
settings.reset_api_key_desc: Please note that resetting your API key requires you to update your .wakatime.cfg files on all your computers.
settings.add_api_keys_desc: Besides the primary (aka. main) API key, which always exists, you can create additional ones with limited permissions.
settings.regenerate_summaries_desc: Regenerate all pre-computed summaries from raw heartbeat data. This is usually only needed after a data import or when summary data appears inconsistent.
settings.clear_data_desc: Clear all your time tracking data from Wakapi. This cannot be undone. Be careful!
settings.change_username_desc: You can change your username, even though it is not recommended. Your old API key will remain valid.
settings.delete_account_desc: Deleting your account will cause all data, including all your heartbeats, to be erased from the server immediately. This action is irreversible.
settings.wakatime_connect_desc: You can connect Wakapi with the official WakaTime (or another Wakapi instance, when optionally specifying a custom API URL) in a way that all heartbeats sent to Wakapi are relayed. This way, you can use both services at the same time. To get started, get
settings.wakatime_forward_desc: To forward data to another Wakapi instance, use
settings.wakatime_privacy_note: Please note: When enabling this feature, the operators of this server will, in theory, have unlimited access to your data stored in WakaTime. If you are concerned about your privacy, please do not enable this integration or wait for OAuth 2 authentication
settings.wakatime_legacy_tooltip: If WakaTime import fails repeatedly, you may want to fall back to an older, less efficient importer mechanism
settings.badges_instructions: This integration allows to generate badges for README pages or forums. To enable this feature, you need to grant public, unauthorized access to the respective endpoints. See
settings.badges_customize: Adapt the URL's label and color parameters for customized badges.
settings.badges_shieldsio: In addition, there is an endpoint compatible with
settings.badges_shieldsio_suffix: to allow for even more customization (e.g. different
settings.badges_public_only: Only available on public instances, not on localhost.
settings.readme_stats_instructions_suffi: to generate fancy cards for you. To enable this feature, you need to grant public, unauthorized access to the respective endpoints. See
settings.readme_stats_7days: Share data for >= 7 days → weekly summary card
settings.readme_stats_366days: Share data for >= 366 days → yearly summary card
settings.readme_stats_alltime: Share all data (-1) → all-time summary card
settings.readme_stats_public_only: Only available on public instances, not on localhost.
settings.heartbeats_timeout_long_desc: This parameter affects the heuristic based on which a series of consecutive heartbeats sent by your IDE are aggregated to total coding time. Please see the "How are durations calculated?" section in our
settings.heartbeats_timeout_long_desc_su: as well as the discussion in
leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
leaderboard.description: Wakapi's leaderboard shows a ranking of the most active users on this server, given they opted in to get listed on the public leaderboard. Statistics are updated at least every 12 hours and are based on the users' total coding time in a pre-defined interval.
leaderboard.participate_hint: To participate, log in, go to
summary.data_retention_warning: Some of your data is older than this instance's data retention period. This will cause old data to be deleted, unless you opt for a subscription. Go to Settings → Subscription for more details.
summary.categories_note: After the category chart was introduced as a new feature in March '24, you will have to run "Settings → Danger Zone → Regenerate Summaries" once to have your categories reflected properly. This may take a few minutes.
summary.hourly_note: Only the last 24 hours within the selected interval are shown in this chart. Use Ctrl + wheel to zoom, hold and click to drag. If there are too many small segments to be displayed, chart remains blank on purpose.
summary.no_data: It looks like there is no data available for the specified time range. If you logged in to Wakapi for the first time, see the setup instructions below on how to get started. Check out Settings → Integrations for instructions on how to use Wakapi and WakaTime in parallel.
summary.data_retention_promo: By default, Wakapi will keep your coding activity for %d months. If you want unlimited data storage or simply support the project (which the authors would very much appreciate), you can opt for a paid subscription.
summary.step1_title: Step 1: Download WakaTime plugin for your IDE
summary.step3_title: Step 3: Start coding and then check back here!
summary.no_data_intro: It looks like there is no data available for the specified time range. If you logged in to Wakapi for the first time, see the setup instructions below on how to get started.
summary.no_data_parallel_suffix: for instructions on how to use Wakapi and WakaTime in parallel.
summary.data_retention_intro: By default, Wakapi will keep your coding activity for %d months. If you want
summary.data_retention_appreciate: (which the authors would very much appreciate),
summary.data_retention_subscribe: you can opt for a paid subscription for
summary.simultaneous_tracking: To track coding activity in Wakapi and WakaTime
filter.label: Label
confirm.cannot_be_undone: Are you sure? This cannot be undone.
confirm.import_no_undo: Are you sure? The import can not be undone.
confirm.clear_data: Are you sure? This can not be undone!
confirm.delete_account: Are you sure? This can not be undone!
projects.title: Your Projects
projects.description: This is an overview of all your projects, ordered by recent activity. Color intensity indicates the overall activity on that project.
projects.no_data: No project data available, yet... Go start coding!
index.title: Keep Track of Your Coding Time
index.subtitle: Wakapi is an open-source tool that helps you keep track of the time you have spent coding on different projects in different programming languages and more. Ideal for statistics freaks and anyone else.
index.total_hours: This system has tracked a total of %s hours of coding from %s users.
index.feature_gdpr_tooltip: Wakapi.dev is hosted in Germany, none of your data will leave the EU
index.tracked_total_prefix: This system has tracked a total of
setup.title: Setup Instructions
setup.subtitle: Get started tracking your coding time with Wakapi in just a few steps – simple and easy!
setup.step1_desc: First step is to download and install the WakaTime plugin into your editor or IDE. Available for Visual Studio Code, PyCharm, IntelliJ, Neovim and many more – even Chrome or Firefox are supported.
setup.step2_desc: After having installed one or more IDE plugins, you will have to adapt the WakaTime config file in your home directory.
setup.step2_edit: Open the file with your favorite editor and adapt the API URL and -key like this:
setup.step3_desc: You're done! Restart your editor or IDE and jump straight into coding. After a short while, you will see your coding activity appear on the dashboard.
reset_password.title: Reset Password
reset_password.subtitle: If you forgot your password, enter the e-mail address you associated with Wakapi to reset it and choose a new one. You will receive an e-mail afterwards. Make sure to check your spam folder.
set_password.title: Choose a new password
set_password.subtitle: You have requested to reset your password. Please choose a new one.
imprint.title: Imprint & Data Privacy
head.title: Wakapi – Coding Statistics
head.description: Wakapi is an open-source tool that helps you keep track of the time you have spent coding on different projects in different programming languages and more.

teams.no_teams: You are not a member of any teams yet.

admin.delete_team_confirm: Delete team %s? This will remove all members. This action cannot be undone.

mail.import_note: You should be able to see the newly imported coding statistics in Wakapi.

mail.report_unsubscribe: If you do not want to receive e-mail reports anymore, please log in to Wakapi and go to Settings to disable them.
mail.reset_body: You have requested to reset your Wakapi password. Please click the following link to proceed.
mail.reset_ignore: If you did not request a password change, please just ignore this mail.
mail.subscription_expiring_body: Your Wakapi subscription will expire soon.
mail.subscription_data_warning: All coding activity older than %d months will be deleted soon. Please refer to this article for further details on subscriptions.
mail.wakatime_failure_body: You have configured Wakapi to relay your heartbeats to WakaTime's API. However, requests for the last %d heartbeats have failed. This is most likely an authentication issue. WakaTime connection is paused for now. To resume it, please re-enter your WakaTime API token under Settings.
